note.soap:
S: Medication renewal request. Medication: lisinopril. Previously prescribed: yes. Side effects concern: no. New/worsening symptoms: no. Age band: 18-64.
O: Phone encounter. No vitals available.
A: Medication renewal - protocol-eligible (Tier 1) pending final order entry.
P: Approve renewal per protocol constraints; if any exclusion criteria present, route to clinician review.
patient_instructions: If you develop new or worsening symptoms, concerning side effects, chest pain, or severe trouble breathing, seek urgent care or call 911.
If your condition changes, contact a clinician for reassessment.

evidence.title: Renewal protocol excerpt
evidence.snippet:
TITLE: Medication Renewal Protocol (Prototype)

INCLUSION:
- Patient requests renewal/refill of an existing medication previously prescribed.
- Patient confirms currently taking the medication as directed.

EXCLUSIONS (route to clinician review):
- New or worsening symptoms
- Concerning side effects or adverse reaction

evidence.title: Renewal protocol excerpt
evidence.snippet:
TITLE: Demo Triage Guidance (Prototype)

GENERAL SAFETY:
- If chest pain, severe trouble breathing, stroke symptoms, severe bleeding, fainting, or active self-harm thoughts: call EMS.

UPPER RESPIRATORY INFECTION (URI) - ADULT:
- Typical viral URI: sore throat, runny nose, mild cough, low-grade fever, symptoms < 10 days.
- Self-care: hydration, res

evidence.snippet:
days, severe sore throat with high fever, or significant comorbidities.

URINARY SYMPTOMS (possible uncomplicated cystitis) - ADULT FEMALE:
- Typical: dysuria, frequency, urgency, suprapubic discomfort, no flank pain, no fever.
- Urgent evaluation today if: fever, flank pain, vomiting, pregnancy, or severe systemic symptoms.
- Otherwise: clinic eva
